title: anthyllis webbiana subsp. arundana
intro: Anthyllis webbiana subsp. arundana, also known as Webb's Anthyllis or Kermes oak, is a plant species belonging to the legume family, Fabaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots are often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
